title: case: patterns, when, exhaustiveness + else
manager: A real `case` with full structural patterns, `when` guards, and — the part that earns its keep — exhaustiveness checking with an explicit `else`. The compiler proves you have handled every possibility of an enum or ADT, so adding a variant later turns every unhandled `case` into a compile error pointing at exactly what you forgot. This is the single biggest defect-prevention feature in the whole expression language.
cs: structural patterns + `when` guards; exhaustiveness over enums (W6.11) and ADTs (W6.20); explicit `else`; a non-exhaustive case without `else` is a compile error.
